title: Reis muted message in 222
wikitext:
[]: Rei's muted message in 2.22             Quote Post by Exaphim » Tue Jul 06, 2010 5:07 pm Rei had something tell Asuka on the phone before the Unit 05 activation test. After she hanged up, Asuka mentioned something about her only wanting to pilot EVA. In my opinion, it probably had something to do with Shinji. That is if you take into account Asuka's reaction (gazing out the window, pep talk with Misato just before going into the entry plug) and the fact that Asuka was being jealous about him and Rei getting closer to begin with. What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Tue Jul 06, 2010 5:16 pm All Rei said was "thank you" after a very long pause. I interpreted it as Rei saying thank you for Asuka piloting Unit-05 during the test so that it wouldn't conflict with the dinner Rei was putting together. Asuka is saying she only wanted to pilot, that it had nothing to do with wanting to be nice to Rei. Of course, we can tell from her small smile that Asuka does in fact care.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Tue Jul 06, 2010 8:52 pm subAtomic wrote: I interpreted it as Rei saying thank you for Asuka piloting Unit-05 during the test so that it wouldn't conflict with the dinner Rei was putting together.

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Tue Jul 06, 2010 9:17 pm Yes, Unit-03. Numbers ain't my strong suite.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Jul 07, 2010 12:25 am No worries

[]:             Quote Post by Exaphim » Wed Jul 07, 2010 2:05 am subAtomic wrote: Yes, Unit-03. Numbers ain't my strong suite.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Wed Jul 07, 2010 2:24 am Exaphim wrote: Guess I really want to see a Shinji x Asuka this time around, but that's not likely to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Hypercube » Wed Jul 07, 2010 9:06 am No Rei said "Shinji is mine B**** you can P*** out now W****" Asuka smiled because she was stunned by Rei's strong and delightful words... Ok i leave.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Jul 07, 2010 9:42 am i saw the title of this thread and immediately went: "what?! anno did it again ?! and i missed it?! noooooooooo...!" then i read on and noticed it was about Rei's "thank you." phew. either way, just want to comment that this was one of my favorite scenes, because it tells us a lot about both girls: Rei is opening up to people in general (as opposed to just Shinji), and Asuka is learning that it's not wrong to open yourself up to people. also, i think there are other layers to what she comes to understand: you shouldn't try and force people's affections because you're you and they have to love you, instead you have to let people come to care about you on their own. and that if you care about someone, you shouldn't be selfish; you should want the best for them (in this case, give Shinji the opportunity to make up with his father). she also learns that Rei has feelings that should be respected, and isn't just a doll. of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Hypercube » Wed Jul 07, 2010 1:26 pm But that will disappoint many people (including me ) who loved the doll-like soulless yet beautiful girl that Rei was.... I guess modifying Rei's character is quite risky...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jul 07, 2010 1:50 pm Hypercube wrote: But that will disappoint many people (including me ) who loved the doll-like soulless yet beautiful girl that Rei was.... I guess modifying Rei's character is quite risky...

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Wed Jul 07, 2010 4:05 pm Sachi wrote: If you haven't noticed, Rei already exhibits un-doll-like behavior throughout all of Eva 2.22.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Wed Jul 07, 2010 4:10 pm Nightquest wrote: .. or in this case, I guess she didn't know how to use the phone, because Ritusko told her when she had to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Wed Jul 07, 2010 4:28 pm Oz wrote: I think you're missing the point with this scene. It was Rei who wanted to call in the first place. Now that's something we have never seen her do before. Lack of technical know-how hardly makes her behavior "clone-like".

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Jul 07, 2010 7:25 pm Nightquest wrote: Yes, I meant, when Ritsuko told her something like "You can speak now, Rei..." a few seconds after the " pip " (?)

[]:             Quote Post by Tycho » Thu Jul 08, 2010 12:09 am Hypercube wrote: But that will disappoint many people (including me ) who loved the doll-like soulless yet beautiful girl that Rei was.... I guess modifying Rei's character is quite risky...

[]:             Quote Post by Hypercube » Thu Jul 08, 2010 11:31 am I guess everyone still remembers the epic moment when she smiled the first time in the series,no? Why? because we liked her character so much that we were like "Omg i wonder how she looks when she smiles?!" In EVA:2.0 she is more "natural",and she express her feelings now (inviting people to her house...) .While in the series we've only seen the robot-like Rei's room;which reflects well her personnality. I am scared now if she really is falling in love with Shinji or not...(never thought that will happen)

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:09 pm I guess maybe because I'm not jaded by 1,000 other anime relationships, but it just seems perfectly obvious to me that Rei has no romantic feelings for Shinji. She cares about him because he's the first person to care about her aside from Gendo, and she has almost a sister/mother affection for him. The only girl in Rebuild who has any feelings for Shinji is Asuka. It seems to me like diehard fans of the original are so used to the emotionless Rei doll, that even a smile or any small indication of humanity might as well be Rei breaking into "(I'm In Love With) A Wonderful Guy" . It might seem hokey to some, but Anno has made a big deal out of sharing a meal in Rebuild. Hot baths, hot meals, these are ways to find peace and tranquility amidst the chaos of daily life. Maybe Anno learned that in therapy or something. He's uses the Miso soup, Shinji's bentos, Rei's dinner, as a way to break through the ice that exists between these kids. It may be different than the series, but it's not altogether unrealistic.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:37 pm would it be so horrible if she fell in love with Shinji? (or anyone, really?). this is the girl who couldn't understand (or wasn't interested in understanding) interpersonal connections, honestly coming to care about someone. i don't ship S/R but IMO it would be somewhat of a milestone for her. let's say she survives everything by the last movie (and the world does, as well)-- i would want her to be able to fall in love with someone, because i like Rei and i want her to be happy. and mind you, how does her not knowing how to use a phone immediately point to her being a clone? she's just never used a phone before. it's a sign that she's somewhat odd, sure, but getting from that to clone ... my grandma doesn't know how to use a cellphone either, you know, and i don't think she's a clone.

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:44 pm carla wrote: would it be so horrible if she fell in love with Shinji?

Article: Reis muted message in 222
1:09 pm I guess maybe because I'm not jaded by 1,000 other anime relationships, but it just seems perfectly obvious to me that Rei has no romantic feelings for Shinji. She cares about him because he's the first person to care about her aside from Gendo, and she has almost a sister/mother affection for him. The only girl in Rebuild who has any feelings for Shinji is Asuka. It seems to me like diehard fans of the original are so used to the emotionless Rei doll, that even a smile or any small indication of humanity might as well be Rei breaking into "(I'm In Love With) A Wonderful Guy" . It might seem hokey to some, but Anno has made a big deal out of sharing a meal in Rebuild. Hot baths, hot meals, these are ways to find peace and tranquility amidst the chaos of daily life. Maybe Anno learned that in therapy or something. He's uses the Miso soup, Shinji's bentos, Rei's dinner, as a way to break through the ice that exists between these kids. It may be different than the series, but it's not altogether unrealistic.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:37 pm would it be so horrible if she fell in love with Shinji? (or anyone, really?). this is the girl who couldn't understand (or wasn't interested in understanding) interpersonal connections, honestly coming to care about someone. i don't ship S/R but IMO it would be somewhat of a milestone for her. let's say she survives everything by the last movie (and the world does, as well)-- i would want her to be able to fall in love with someone, because i like Rei and i want her to be happy. and mind you, how does her not knowing how to use a phone immediately point to her being a clone? she's just never used a phone before. it's a sign that she's somewhat odd, sure, but getting from that to clone ... my grandma doesn't know how to use a cellphone either, you know, and i don't think she's a clone.

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:44 pm carla wrote: would it be so horrible if she fell in love with Shinji?
